Implementation is consistent and complete: the contract descriptor (review_fixture_tail_descriptor, DmsCoreCp233ReviewFixtureTailDescriptor) binds to CP00-233 and chains to CP00-232; all 7 required public exports exist (2 in service.js, 5 in validators.js) and are re-exported via index.js `export *`; the README has a correct ## CP00-233 section; and the validate script plus model.test.js cover coverage, descriptor-only rows, authority boundary, evidence packets, and handoff. Per command-evidence.json, rp06:dms-core:validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run build, and git diff --check all returned exit 0. No-write and no-real-data boundaries are extensively attested in the contract (cp233_no_write_attestation + per-descriptor no_write_attestation) and enforced in code via freezeCp233Result, which hardcodes synthetic_only/no_real_data and all no-runtime flags; content is synthetic descriptor metadata with no real client/matter/document data. Hermes is used only for descriptor-only evidence packets (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; receipt_shape descriptor_only_..._no_write). production_ready posture is correct and not over-claimed: manifest production_ready=false, only production_ready_candidate is emitted by the Hermes packet. The review harness is correctly hardened: model claude-opus-4-8/effort max/read_only, readiness auth preflight passed with well-formed JSON, summary_only_review prohibited (security/review deliverables present), no forbidden tools. One non-blocking P3 traceability nit: the M05 documentation_update descriptor row points to README#cp00-231 instead of #cp00-233 (reused DMS_CORE_CP231_ROW_EXTRAS); the descriptor-level anchor and the README section are correct, no validator depends on it, and it has no runtime/boundary impact.","findings":[{"id":"CP233-P3-001","severity":"P3","title":"Stale documentation_entry anchor in M05 documentation_update descriptor row (#cp00-231 instead of #cp00-233)","file":"packages/dms/src/service.js","line":5610,"details":"The CP00-233 review-fixture-tail case-set generator (service.js:5863) reuses DMS_CORE_CP231_ROW_EXTRAS, defined at service.js:5608-5613, whose documentation_update extra hardcodes documentation_entry='packages/dms/README.md#cp00-231' (service.js:5610). As a result the generated CP00-233 descriptor's RP06.P09.M05 documentation_update row points to the CP00-231 README section, and this is mirrored in the committed contract projection at contracts/dms-core-contract.json:57047. Every other pack's documentation_update row points to its own anchor (CP00-230->#cp00-230 at contract:54546, CP00-231->#cp00-231 at contract:55377), so CP00-233 should point to #cp00-233. This is a descriptor-only metadata/traceability inconsistency: the descriptor-level documentation_entry is correctly 'packages/dms/README.md#cp00-233' (service.js:5891, contract:57416), the README contains a correct '## CP00-233' section (packages/dms/README.md:149-151), no validator inspects row-level documentation_entry (validators.js has no reference to it, so rp06:dms-core:validate passes), and there is no runtime, no-write, no-leak, authority-boundary, coverage, or production_ready impact.","recommendation":"Introduce a CP233-specific row-extras constant (or override documentation_update.documentation_entry to 'packages/dms/README.md#cp00-233') so the M05 tail row points to the CP00-233 README section, mirroring the fix into contracts/dms-core-contract.json:57047. Optionally add a validator assertion that each pack's documentation_update anchor matches its own pack id to prevent future template carryover. Suitable for a future descriptor-cleanup pack; not required for CP00-233 closeout.","post_review_disposition":"Non-blocking (P3).
